question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a query amplification DoS by sending requests that trigger slow DB queries, knowing that statementTimeout is the only throttle and it applies per-statement rather than per-connection or per-user?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the #nosec G118 suppression comment (line 35) which disables the gosec linter warning for context.WithTimeout, potentially hiding a real context leak vulnerability that was suppressed without proper justification?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that GetDbWithContext() does not validate that statementTimeout is within a safe range, allowing a misconfigured very large timeout (e.g., MaxInt) to effectively disable the timeout protection?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a goroutine leak by triggering many requests where the returned *gorm.DB from GetDbWithContext() is used but the CancelFunc is never deferred, accumulating live child contexts until memory is exhausted?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that GetDbWithContext() returns the raw d.db (line 24) without a context when statementTimeout <= 0 and ctx is nil, meaning queries run with no deadline and no cancellation, enabling indefinite query execution?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: GetDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that GetDb() returns the raw *gorm.DB instance (line 19) with no context or timeout attached, allowing any caller to execute unbounded queries that hold DB connections open indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: GetDb()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a DoS by exploiting API endpoints that use GetDb() instead of GetDbWithContext(), since GetDb() bypasses the statementTimeout mechanism entirely?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: GetDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unrestricted access to the underlying *gorm.DB via GetDb() to cause connection pool starvation if a caller opens a transaction and never commits or rolls it back?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NewDbClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that NewDbClient() accepts a statementTimeout of 0 or negative without validation (line 40-42), silently disabling all query timeouts and enabling long-running query DoS attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NewDbClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that NewDbClient() does not validate that the provided *gorm.DB is non-nil, so if ConnectToDb() passes a nil db due to an error path, NewDbClient() creates a client wrapping nil that panics on first use?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DSN format string in GetDsn() (host=%s port=%d user=%s dbname=%s password=%s sslmode=disable) where if any string field contains spaces or special characters, it could alter the DSN parsing by the PostgreSQL driver?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence the dbConfig (e.g., via environment variable injection) inject a crafted Host value containing spaces and additional DSN key=value pairs to override sslmode or other connection parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Password field in GetDsn() is interpolated directly into the DSN string without escaping, so a password containing spaces or '=' characters could corrupt the DSN and cause unexpected connection behavior?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Username field in GetDsn() is not sanitized, so a username containing DSN metacharacters could inject additional connection parameters into the PostgreSQL connection string?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Host field in GetDsn() is not validated or sanitized, allowing a crafted host value to redirect the DB connection to an attacker-controlled server that captures credentials?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of certificate verification (sslmode=disable) to perform SSL stripping on the DB connection, downgrading any attempted TLS negotiation to plaintext?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() does not set a connection timeout on the underlying sql.DB, causing the application to hang indefinitely during DB reconnection attempts triggered by connection drops?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a slow-startup DoS by making the DB unreachable at startup time, since ConnectToDb() has no timeout on gorm.Open() and will block the application initialization indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that sqlDb.SetConnMaxLifetime() uses time.Duration(dbConfig.Pool.MaxLifetime) * time.Minute (line 32), and if MaxLifetime is set to a very large integer, it overflows the Duration type, resulting in a negative or zero lifetime that disables connection recycling?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit integer overflow in time.Duration(dbConfig.Pool.MaxLifetime) * time.Minute where MaxLifetime is a large int value, causing the computed duration to wrap around to a negative value and SetConnMaxLifetime() to behave unexpectedly?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that there is no upper bound on dbConfig.Pool.MaxOpenConnections, allowing it to be set to math.MaxInt32, which causes the sql.DB pool to attempt to open an unbounded number of connections and exhaust OS file descriptors?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() does not ping the database after connecting (no db.Ping()), so a successful-looking connection may actually be broken, and the first real query will fail with an error that is not handled gracefully?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of connection health checks in ConnectToDb() to cause the application to serve requests using a stale or broken DB connection pool, resulting in cascading query failures?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that log.Errorf('Failed to get sql DB: %s', err) (line 27) may include internal error details from the PostgreSQL driver that reveal schema names, table names, or connection parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: '] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() returns nil when db.DB() fails (line 29), and if the caller does not check for nil before using the returned DbClient, a nil pointer dereference panic occurs, crashing the service?
